title: galium floribundum subsp. floribundum
intro: Galium floribundum subsp. floribundum, also known as sweet woodruff, is a perennial plant native to Europe and parts of Asia, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Galium floribundum subsp. floribundum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Herbal preparations of sweet woodruff can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote relaxation and improve overall well-being.
